classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.acryl_cs_issues.models.DataHubProductGlossary
documentation:
This class defines the DataHub Product Glossary.
Eventually we can move it to a real business glossary implementation in
DataHub
For now, we will have classes (representing product concerns) - like
GlossaryNodes
Within those classes, we will have terms (representing product features,
issue categories etc.). These would be the glossary terms conceptually.

Any time we change this class (or internal classes), we should increment
the version number to ensure that the changes are accounted for in the
summarization process.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.acryl_cs_issues.models.Issue.last_activity
documentation:
Returns the last activity time on the issue. This can be the last time
the issue was updated or the last time a comment was made on the issue.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.acryl_cs_issues.acryl_slack.AcrylSlack.get_image_data_url
documentation:
Fetch image data from Slack and convert it to a data URL.

```
param private_url

The private Slack image URL

param slack_token

Your Slack API token

return

A data URL containing the image data
```

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.acryl_cs_issues.acryl_slack.AcrylSlack.get_slack_conversations_from_text
documentation:
Extracts Slack conversation URLs and list of messages from the given
text per URL.

If no conversation URLs are found, an empty dictionary is returned.

Example Slack URLs:
- https://acryldata.slack.com/archives/C06LH87S6SG/p1721920964955729?thread_ts=1721443902.665799&cid=C06LH87S6SG
- https://acryldata.slack.com/archives/C06LH87S6SG/p1721922184323209

Args:
text (str): The text to search for Slack URLs.

Returns:
Dict[str, Union[bool, str, List[TicketComment]]]: A dictionary
containing
- is_thread (bool): Whether the conversation is a thread
- url (str): The URL of the conversation
- messages (List[TicketComment]): A list of messages in the conversation

TODO-s:
- Add support for detecting User mentions even when there is no Slack
URL in the text and use that to hunt for messages in Slack to link to
the ticket (This is important because there are many cases where the
agent will create a ticket from Slack and not provide the URL of the
Slack conversation)
- Make the return type a dataclass

functions.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.acryl_cs_issues.acryl_customer.needs_properties_update
documentation:
Check if the issue properties needs to be updated in the DataHub graph.
1. If the issue has been updated since the last time it was updated in
the graph
2. If the summary algorithm version has changed
3. If the length of the conversation list has changed
4. If the glossary version has changed

functions.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.acryl_cs_issues.acryl_customer.get_linear_issue_url_from_comment
documentation:
Comment body looks like: Issue CUS-2296 was linked to the ticket
Return the issue id from the comment. (in this case, CUS-2296)
URL to the comment: https://linear.app/acryl-data/issue/CUS-2296

functions.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.acryl_cs_issues.acryl_customer.generate_markdown_with_logo
documentation:
Generate a markdown string with an inline logo next to the author and date.

```
param author

The name of the author

param date

A datetime object representing the date

param logo_url

The URL of the logo image

param logo_size

The size (width and height) of the logo in pixels

return

A markdown string with the inline logo and text
```
https://linear.app/cdn-cgi/imagedelivery/fO02fVwohEs9s9UHFwon6A/82d07241-84b3-4cdf-33b5-a09b8d169300/f=auto,dpr=2,q=95,fit=scale-down,metadata=none

modules.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes
documentation: mypy: ignore-errors
flake8: noqa
This file is autogenerated by /metadata-ingestion/scripts/avro_codegen.py
Do not modify manually!
pylint: skip-file
fmt: off
isort: skip_file
The SchemaFromJSONData method only exists in avro-python3, but is called make_avsc_object in avro.
We can use this fact to detect conflicts between the two packages. Pip won't detect those conflicts
because both are namespace packages, and hence are allowed to overwrite files from each other.
This means that installation order matters, which is a pretty unintuitive outcome.
See https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/4625 for details.

documentation: A unique identifier for the message

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.KafkaAuditHeaderClass.auditVersion
documentation: The version that is being used for auditing. In version 0, the audit trail buckets events into 10 minute audit windows based on the EventHeader timestamp. In version 1, the audit trail buckets events as follows: if the schema has an outer KafkaAuditHeader, use the outer audit header timestamp for bucketing; else if the EventHeader has an inner KafkaAuditHeader use that inner audit header's timestamp for bucketing

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.KafkaAuditHeaderClass.fabricUrn
documentation: The fabricUrn of the host from which the event is being emitted. Fabric Urn in the format of urn:li:fabric:{fabric_name}. See go/fabric.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.KafkaAuditHeaderClass.clusterConnectionString
documentation: This is a String that the client uses to establish some kind of connection with the Kafka cluster. The exact format of it depends on specific versions of clients and brokers. This information could potentially identify the fabric and cluster with which the client is producing to or consuming from.
This header records information about the context of an event as it is emitted into kafka and is intended to be used by the kafka audit application.  For more information see go/kafkaauditheader

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.AssertionStdOperatorClass.NOT_IN

documentation:
A freshness assertion, or an assertion which indicates when a particular operation should occur

documentation:
A volume assertion, or an assertion which indicates how much data should be available for a
particular asset.

documentation:
A schema or structural assertion.

Would have named this SCHEMA but the codegen for PDL does not allow this (reserved word).

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.AssertionTypeClass.CUSTOM
documentation:
A custom assertion. 
When this is the value, the customAssertion field will be populated.
Use this assertion type when the exact type of assertion is not modeled in DataHub or
as a starting point when integrating third-party data quality tools.
Type of assertion. Assertion types can evolve to span Datasets, Flows (Pipelines), Models, Features etc.

documentation:
The definition of an assertion that validates individual values of a field / column for a set of rows.
This type of assertion verifies that each column value meets a particular requirement.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FieldAssertionInfoClass.fieldMetricAssertion
documentation:
The definition of an assertion that validates a common metric obtained about a field / column for a set of rows.
This type of assertion verifies that the value of a high-level metric obtained by aggregating over a column meets
expectations

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FieldAssertionInfoClass.filter
documentation:
A definition of the specific filters that should be applied, when performing monitoring.
If not provided, there is no filter, and the full table is under consideration.
If using DataHub Dataset Profiles as the assertion source type, the value of this field will be ignored.
Attributes defining a Field Assertion.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FieldAssertionTypeClass.FIELD_VALUES

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FieldAssertionTypeClass.FIELD_METRIC
documentation:
An assertion used to validate the value of a common field / column metric (e.g. aggregation) such as null count + percentage,
min, max, median, and more.

documentation:

evaluate the assertion against the metric value.

documentation: Standard parameters required for the assertion. e.g. min_value, max_value, value, columns

Attributes defining a field metric assertion, which asserts an expectation against
a common metric derived from the set of field / column values, for example:
max, min, median, null count, null percentage, unique count, unique percentage, and more.

Note: This is a completely different metric different from NULL_PERCENTAGE!

The type of the field transform, e.g. the transformation
function / operator to apply.

Definition of a transform applied to the values of a column / field.
Note that the applicability of a field transform ultimately depends on the native type
of the field / column.

Model has single field to permit extension.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FieldTransformTypeClass.LENGTH

An optional transform to apply to field values
before evaluating the operator.

If none is applied, the field value will be compared as is.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FieldValuesAssertionClass.operator
documentation:
The predicate to evaluate against a single value of the field.
Depending on the operator, parameters may be required in order to successfully
evaluate the assertion against the field value.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FieldValuesAssertionClass.parameters
documentation: Standard parameters required for the assertion. e.g. min_value, max_value, value, columns

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FieldValuesAssertionClass.failThreshold
documentation:
Additional customization about when the assertion
should be officially considered failing.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FieldValuesAssertionClass.excludeNulls
documentation:
Whether to ignore or allow nulls when running the values assertion. (i.e.
consider only non-null values) using operators OTHER than the IS_NULL operator.

Defaults to true, allowing null values.

Attributes defining a field values assertion, which asserts that the values for a field / column
of a dataset / table matches a set of expectations.

In other words, this type of assertion acts as a semantic constraint applied to fields for a specific column.

TODO: We should display the "failed row count" to the user if the column fails the verification rules.
TODO: Determine whether we need an "operator" that can be applied to the field.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FieldValuesFailThresholdClass.type
documentation:
The type of failure threshold. Either based on the number
of column values (rows) that fail the expectations, or the percentage
of the total rows under consideration.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FieldValuesFailThresholdClass.value
documentation:
By default this is 0, meaning that ALL column values (i.e. rows) must
meet the defined expectations.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FreshnessAssertionScheduleClass.cron
documentation: A cron schedule. This field is required when type is CRON.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FreshnessAssertionScheduleClass.fixedInterval
documentation: A fixed interval schedule. This field is required when type is FIXED_INTERVAL.
Attributes defining a single Freshness schedule.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FreshnessAssertionScheduleTypeClass.CRON
documentation:
A highly configurable recurring schedule which describes the times of events described
by a CRON schedule, with the evaluation schedule assuming to be matching the cron schedule.

In a CRON schedule type, we compute the look-back window to be the time between the last scheduled event
and the current event (evaluation time). This means that the evaluation schedule must match exactly
the schedule defined inside the cron schedule.

For example, a CRON schedule defined as "0 8 * * *" would represent a schedule of "every day by 8am". Assuming
that the assertion evaluation schedule is defined to match this, the freshness assertion would be evaluated in the following way:

    1. Compute the "last scheduled occurrence" of the event using the CRON schedule. For example, yesterday at 8am.
    2. Compute the bounds of a time window between the "last scheduled occurrence" (yesterday at 8am) until the "current occurrence" (today at 8am)
    3. Verify that the target event has occurred within the CRON-interval window.
    4. If the target event has occurred within the time window, then assertion passes.
    5. If the target event has not occurred within the time window, then the assertion fails.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FreshnessAssertionScheduleTypeClass.FIXED_INTERVAL
documentation:
A fixed interval which is used to compute a look-back window for use when evaluating the assertion relative
to the Evaluation Time of the Assertion.

To compute the valid look-back window, we subtract the fixed interval from the evaluation time. Then, we verify
that the target event has occurred within that window.

For example, a fixed interval of "24h" would represent a schedule of "in the last 24 hours".
The 24 hour interval is relative to the evaluation time of the assertion. For example if we schedule the assertion
to be evaluated each hour, we'd compute the result as follows:

    1. Subtract the fixed interval from the current time (Evaluation time) to compute the bounds of a fixed look-back window.
    2. Verify that the target event has occurred within the look-back window.
    3. If the target event has occurred within the time window, then assertion passes.
    4. If the target event has not occurred within the time window, then the assertion fails.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FreshnessAssertionScheduleTypeClass.SINCE_THE_LAST_CHECK
documentation:
A stateful check that takes the last time this check ran to determine the look-back window.

To compute the valid look-back- window, we start at the time the monitor last evaluated this assertion,
and we end at the point in time the check is currently running.

For example, let's say a Freshness assertion is of type SINCE_THE_LAST_CHECK, and the monitor is configured to
run every day at 12:00am. Let's assume this assertion was last evaluated yesterday at 12:04am. We'd compute
the result as follows:

    1. Get the timestamp for the last run of the monitor on this assertion.
    2. look_back_window_start_time = latest_monitor_run.timestampMillis [ie. 12:04a yesterday]
    3. look_back_window_end_time = nowMillis [ie. 12:02a today]
    4. If the target event has occurred within the window [ie. 12:04a yday to 12:02a today],
       then the assertion passes.
    5. If the target event has not occurred within the window, then the assertion fails.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FreshnessAssertionTypeClass.DATASET_CHANGE
documentation:
An Freshness based on Operations performed on a particular Dataset (insert, update, delete, etc) and sourced from an audit log, as
opposed to based on the highest watermark in a timestamp column (e.g. a query). Only valid when entity is of type "dataset".

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FreshnessAssertionTypeClass.DATA_JOB_RUN
documentation: An Freshness based on a successful execution of a Data Job.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FreshnessCronScheduleClass.windowStartOffsetMs
documentation:
An optional offset in milliseconds to SUBTRACT from the timestamp generated by the cron schedule
to generate the lower bounds of the "freshness window", or the window of time in which an event must have occurred in order for the Freshness check

If left empty, the start of the SLA window will be the _end_ of the previously evaluated Freshness window.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FreshnessFieldKindClass.HIGH_WATERMARK

If rows with this column have not been added since the previous check
then the Freshness Assertion will fail.

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.IncrementingSegmentSpecClass

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.IncrementingSegmentSpecClass.field
documentation: The field to use to generate segments. It must be constantly incrementing as new rows are inserted.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.IncrementingSegmentSpecClass.transformer
documentation:
Optional transformer function to apply to the field in order to obtain the final segment or bucket identifier.
If not provided, then no operator will be applied to the field. (identity function)

Core attributes required to identify an incrementing segment in a table. This type is mainly useful
for tables that constantly increase with new rows being added on a particular cadence (e.g. fact or event tables)

An incrementing segment represents a logical chunk of data which is INSERTED
into a dataset on a regular interval, along with the presence of a constantly-incrementing column
value such as an event time, date partition, or last modified column.

An incrementing segment is principally identified by 2 key attributes combined:

 1. A field or column that represents the incrementing value. New rows that are inserted will be identified using this column.
    Note that the value of this column may not by itself represent the "bucket" or the "segment" in which the row falls.

 2. [Optional] An transformer function that may be applied to the selected column value in order
    to obtain the final "segment identifier" or "bucket identifier". Rows that have the same value after applying the transformation
    will be grouped into the same segment, using which the final value (e.g. row count) will be determined.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.VolumeAssertionTypeClass.ROW_COUNT_TOTAL
documentation: A volume assertion that is evaluated against the total row count of a dataset.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.VolumeAssertionTypeClass.ROW_COUNT_CHANGE
documentation:

or a row count change.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.VolumeAssertionTypeClass.INCREMENTING_SEGMENT_ROW_COUNT_TOTAL
documentation:
A volume assertion that checks the latest "segment" in a table based on an incrementing
column to check whether it's row count falls into a particular range.

This can be used to monitor the row count of an incrementing date-partition column segment.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.VolumeAssertionTypeClass.INCREMENTING_SEGMENT_ROW_COUNT_CHANGE
documentation:
A volume assertion that compares the row counts in neighboring "segments" or "partitions"
of an incrementing column.
This can be used to track changes between subsequent date partition
in a table, for example.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.BrowsePathsV2Class.path
documentation:
A valid browse path for the entity. This field is provided by DataHub by default.
This aspect is a newer version of browsePaths where we can encode more information in the path.
This path is also based on containers for a given entity if it has containers.

This is stored in elasticsearch as unit-separator delimited strings and only includes platform specific folders or containers.
These paths should not include high level info captured elsewhere ie. Platform and Environment.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ChangeAuditStampsClass.deleted
documentation: An AuditStamp corresponding to the deletion of this resource/association/sub-resource. Logically, deleted MUST have a later timestamp than creation. It may or may not have the same time as lastModified depending upon the resource/association/sub-resource semantics.

Data captured on a resource/association/sub-resource level giving insight into when that resource/association/sub-resource moved into various lifecycle stages, and who acted to move it into those lifecycle stages. The recommended best practice is to include this record in your record schema, and annotate its fields as @readOnly in your resource. See https://github.com/linkedin/rest.li/wiki/Validation-in-Rest.li#restli-validation-annotations

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DocumentationClass.documentations
documentation: Documentations associated with this asset. We could be receiving docs from different sources

Aspect used for storing all applicable documentations on assets.
This aspect supports multiple documentations from different sources.
There is an implicit assumption that there is only one documentation per
   source.
For example, if there are two documentations from the same source, the
   latest one will overwrite the previous one.
If there are two documentations from different sources, both will be
   stored.
Future evolution considerations:
The first entity that uses this aspect is Schema Field. We will expand this
    aspect to other entities eventually.
The values of the documentation are not currently searchable. This will be
    changed once this aspect develops opinion on which documentation entry is
    the authoritative one.
Ensuring that there is only one documentation per source is a business
    rule that is not enforced by the aspect yet. This will currently be enforced by the
    application that uses this aspect. We will eventually enforce this rule in
    the aspect using AspectMutators.

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLFeatureDataTypeClass

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLFeatureDataTypeClass.USELESS
documentation:
Useless data is unique, discrete data with no potential relationship with the outcome variable.
A useless feature has high cardinality. An example would be bank account numbers that were generated randomly.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLFeatureDataTypeClass.NOMINAL
documentation:
Nominal data is made of discrete values with no numerical relationship between the different categories - mean and median are meaningless.
Animal species is one example. For example, pig is not higher than bird and lower than fish.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLFeatureDataTypeClass.ORDINAL
documentation:
Ordinal data are discrete integers that can be ranked or sorted.
For example, the distance between first and second may not be the same as the distance between second and third.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLFeatureDataTypeClass.BINARY
documentation: Binary data is discrete data that can be in only one of two categories - either yes or no, 1 or 0, off or on, etc

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLFeatureDataTypeClass.COUNT
documentation:
Count data is discrete whole number data - no negative numbers here.
Count data often has many small values, such as zero and one.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLFeatureDataTypeClass.TIME
documentation:
Time data is a cyclical, repeating continuous form of data.
The relevant time features can be any period- daily, weekly, monthly, annual, etc.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLFeatureDataTypeClass.INTERVAL
documentation:
Interval data has equal spaces between the numbers and does not represent a temporal pattern.
Examples include percentages, temperatures, and income.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLFeatureDataTypeClass.IMAGE
documentation: Image Data

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLFeatureDataTypeClass.VIDEO
documentation: Video Data

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLFeatureDataTypeClass.AUDIO
documentation: Audio Data

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLFeatureDataTypeClass.TEXT
documentation: Text Data

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLFeatureDataTypeClass.MAP
documentation: Mapping Data Type ex: dict, map

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLFeatureDataTypeClass.SEQUENCE
documentation: Sequence Data Type ex: list, tuple, range

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLFeatureDataTypeClass.SET
documentation: Set Data Type ex: set, frozenset

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLFeatureDataTypeClass.CONTINUOUS
documentation: Continuous data are made of uncountable values, often the result of a measurement such as height, weight, age etc.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLFeatureDataTypeClass.BYTE
documentation: Bytes data are binary-encoded values that can represent complex objects.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLFeatureDataTypeClass.UNKNOWN
documentation: Unknown data are data that we don't know the type for.
MLFeature Data Type

documentation: Type of content the Media is storing, e.g. image, video, etc.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MediaClass.location
documentation: Where the media content is stored.

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MediaTypeClass

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MediaTypeClass.IMAGE
documentation: The Media holds an image.
Enum defining the type of content a Media object holds.

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MetadataAttributionClass

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MetadataAttributionClass.time
documentation: When this metadata was updated.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MetadataAttributionClass.actor
documentation:
The entity (e.g. a member URN) responsible for applying the assocated metadata. This can
either be a user (in case of UI edits) or the datahub system for automation.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MetadataAttributionClass.source
documentation:
The DataHub source responsible for applying the associated metadata. This will only be filled out
when a DataHub source is responsible. This includes the specific metadata test urn, the automation urn.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MetadataAttributionClass.sourceDetail
documentation:
The details associated with why this metadata was applied. For example, this could include
the actual regex rule, sql statement, ingestion pipeline ID, etc.
Also can include flags like 'propagated'=true or 'inferred'=true.
Information about who, why, and how this metadata was applied

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MetadataProducerTypeClass

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MetadataProducerTypeClass.INGESTION_SOURCE
documentation: Operation was produced during a normal ingestion source run.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MetadataProducerTypeClass.MONITOR
documentation: A running monitor produced the metadata.
The type of the producer of the metadata, e.g. reflective of the process that produced the metadata.

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationClass

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationClass.timestampMillis
documentation: The event timestamp field as epoch at UTC in milli seconds.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationClass.eventGranularity
documentation: Granularity of the event if applicable

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationClass.partitionSpec
documentation: The optional partition specification.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationClass.messageId
documentation: The optional messageId, if provided serves as a custom user-defined unique identifier for an aspect value.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationClass.actor
documentation: Actor who issued this operation.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationClass.operationType
documentation: Operation type of change.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationClass.customOperationType
documentation: A custom type of operation. Required if operationType is CUSTOM.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationClass.numAffectedRows
documentation: How many rows were affected by this operation.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationClass.affectedDatasets
documentation: Which other datasets were affected by this operation.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationClass.sourceType
documentation:
Source Type - E.g. which process produced this operation. Note that this field will be superseded by the richer information
provided inside the "source" field (e.g source > producerType)

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationClass.source
documentation: More detailed information about the source of the operation.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationClass.customProperties
documentation: Custom properties

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationClass.lastUpdatedTimestamp
documentation: The time at which the operation occurred. Would be better named 'operationTime'
Operational info for an entity.

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationSourceDetailsClass

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationSourceDetailsClass.producerType
documentation: The specific type of producer for the operation.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationSourceDetailsClass.producerUrn
documentation:
The producer urn for the operation. Only provided is producerType == MONITOR currently,
where it will be set to the monitor urn that sampled the operation. Ideally, this also contains
the ingestion source urn if applicable.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationSourceDetailsClass.originType
documentation: The specific origin of the operation with regards to the source platform.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationSourceDetailsClass.field
documentation: Information about the field / column. May be present when originType is FIELD_VALUE
Additional details about the origins of a captured source-platform Operation.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationSourceOriginTypeClass.FIELD_VALUE
documentation: Operation was identified by querying a particular field / column value.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationSourceOriginTypeClass.INFORMATION_SCHEMA
documentation: Operation was identified by inspecting an information schema table, or other system metadata table.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationSourceOriginTypeClass.AUDIT_LOG
documentation: Operation was identified by inspecting an audit log API

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationSourceOriginTypeClass.FILE_METADATA
documentation: Operation was identified by inspecting underlying file system.

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationSourceTypeClass

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationSourceTypeClass.DATA_PROCESS
documentation: Provided by a Data Process

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationSourceTypeClass.DATA_PLATFORM
documentation: Update was provided by consulting the data platform  itself.
The source of an operation. Note that this may be superceded by sourceDetails > producerType.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationTypeClass.INSERT
documentation: Rows were inserted

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationTypeClass.UPDATE
documentation: Rows were updated

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationTypeClass.DELETE

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationTypeClass.CREATE

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationTypeClass.ALTER
documentation: Asset was altered

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationTypeClass.DROP
documentation: Asset was dropped

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OperationTypeClass.CUSTOM
documentation: Custom asset operation
Enum to define the operation type when an entity changes.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OriginClass.externalType

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OriginClass.sourceDetails
documentation: Details about the source(s) by which this asset was originated
Carries information about where an entity originated from.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OriginTypeClass.NATIVE
documentation: The entity is native to DataHub.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OriginTypeClass.EXTERNAL
documentation: The entity is external to DataHub.
Enum to define where an entity originated from.

documentation:
Owner URN, e.g. urn:li:corpuser:ldap, urn:li:corpGroup:group_name, and urn:li:multiProduct:mp_name
(Caveat: only corpuser is currently supported in the frontend.)

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnerClass.type
documentation: The type of the ownership

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnerClass.typeUrn
documentation:
The type of the ownership
Urn of type O

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnerClass.source
documentation: Source information for the ownership
Ownership information

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipClass.lastModified

Ownership information of an entity.

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipPromptResponseClass

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipPromptResponseClass.owners
documentation: The owners applied to an asset from this prompt response

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipPromptResponseClass.ownershipTypeUrn
documentation: The type of ownership applied from this prompt response
Response for an ownership type prompt

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipSourceClass

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipSourceClass.type
documentation: The type of the source

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipSourceClass.url
documentation: A reference URL for the source
Source/provider of the ownership information

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipSourceTypeClass.AUDIT
documentation: Auditing system or audit logs

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipSourceTypeClass.DATABASE
documentation: Database, e.g. GRANTS table

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipSourceTypeClass.FILE_SYSTEM
documentation: File system, e.g. file/directory owner

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipSourceTypeClass.ISSUE_TRACKING_SYSTEM
documentation: Issue tracking system, e.g. Jira

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipSourceTypeClass.MANUAL
documentation: Manually provided by a user

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipSourceTypeClass.SERVICE
documentation: Other ownership-like service, e.g. Nuage, ACL service etc

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipSourceTypeClass.SOURCE_CONTROL
documentation: SCM system, e.g. GIT, SVN

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipSourceTypeClass.OTHER
documentation: Other sources

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipTypeClass

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipTypeClass.CUSTOM
documentation:
Set when ownership type is unknown or a when new one is specified as an ownership type entity for which we have no
enum value for. This is used for backwards compatibility

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipTypeClass.TECHNICAL_OWNER
documentation: person or group who is responsible for technical aspects of the asset.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipTypeClass.BUSINESS_OWNER
documentation: A person or group who is responsible for logical, or business related, aspects of the asset.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipTypeClass.DATA_STEWARD
documentation: A steward, expert, or delegate responsible for the asset.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipTypeClass.NONE
documentation: No specific type associated to the owner.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipTypeClass.DEVELOPER
documentation:
A person or group that is in charge of developing the code
Deprecated! Use TECHNICAL_OWNER instead.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipTypeClass.DATAOWNER
documentation:
A person or group that is owning the data
Deprecated! Use TECHNICAL_OWNER instead.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipTypeClass.DELEGATE
documentation:
A person or a group that overseas the operation, e.g. a DBA or SRE.
Deprecated! Use TECHNICAL_OWNER instead.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipTypeClass.PRODUCER
documentation:
A person, group, or service that produces/generates the data
Deprecated! Use TECHNICAL_OWNER instead.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipTypeClass.CONSUMER
documentation:
A person, group, or service that consumes the data
Deprecated! Use TECHNICAL_OWNER or BUSINESS_OWNER instead.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.OwnershipTypeClass.STAKEHOLDER
documentation:
A person or a group that has direct business interest
Deprecated! Use TECHNICAL_OWNER, BUSINESS_OWNER, or STEWARD instead.
Asset owner types

Proposals aspect for proposed tags and glossary terms for an entity.
These are secondary indices and ActionRequests remain the source of truth.

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SerializedValueClass

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SerializedValueClass.blob
documentation: The serialized blob value.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SerializedValueClass.contentType
documentation: The content-type of the serialized blob value.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SerializedValueClass.schemaType
documentation:
The schema type for the schema that models the object that was serialized
       into the blob.
Absence of this field indicates that the schema is not known.
If the schema is known, the value should be set to the appropriate schema
type.
Use the NONE value if the existing schema categories do not apply.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SerializedValueClass.schemaRef
documentation:
An optional reference to the schema that models the object.
e.g., 'com.linkedin.pegasus2avro.platformresource.slack.SlackConversation'
Captures the serialized value of a (usually) schema-d blob.

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareClass

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareClass.lastShareResults
documentation: The last share result for each destination that this entity has been shared with

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareClass.lastUnshareResults
documentation:
The last unshare result for each destination that this entity was
previously shared with. A successfully unshared destination should not
appear in the lastShareResults field.
Aspect used for keeping track of metadata sharing info

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareConfigClass.enableUpstreamLineage
documentation: Upstream lineage was shared

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareConfigClass.enableDownstreamLineage
documentation: Downstream lineage was shared

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareResultClass

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareResultClass.destination
documentation: Urn of the DataHubConnection related to another Acryl instance

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareResultClass.implicitShareEntity
documentation:
If this share happened implicitly due to another entity being shared, this field
will be populated with that entity urn. Example: if a dataset is shared, its containers
will also be shared. The share result for the container will have the dataset urn here.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareResultClass.created
documentation: Audit stamp for when the first share request for the given destination occurred.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareResultClass.lastAttempt
documentation:
When did we get this share result and who requested the share. This will be the time
that we try and execute the share on the backend, regardless of success.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareResultClass.lastAttemptRequestId
documentation:
The request id that generated this share result. This is the id of the request that triggered this share.
This is primarily used for debugging purposes.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareResultClass.shareConfig
documentation: The config for this particular share result

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareResultClass.lastSuccess
documentation: Audit stamp for the last time a share to this above destination successfully occurred

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareResultClass.status
documentation: Status for this share result - whether or not it succeeded

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareResultClass.statusLastUpdated
documentation:
The last time the status was updated. Timestamp in milliseconds since epoch.
Useful for monitoring if the status is stale.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareResultClass.message
documentation:
Optional message for this share result. Error messages will be stored here.
This message will be presented to the end user, so it should not include stack traces etc.
Info about the results from sharing metadata for a given entity

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareResultStateClass.RUNNING
documentation: The share request is still in progress

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareResultStateClass.SUCCESS
documentation: The share was successful

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareResultStateClass.PARTIAL_SUCCESS
documentation: The share was partially successful. Some, but not all, aspects were shared.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ShareResultStateClass.FAILURE
documentation: The share was a failure

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DashboardUsageStatisticsClass.lastViewedAt
documentation:
Last viewed at

This should not be set in cases where statistics are windowed.

Experimental (Subject to breaking change) -- Stats corresponding to dashboard's usage.

If this aspect represents the latest snapshot of the statistics about a Dashboard, the eventGranularity field should be null. 
If this aspect represents a bucketed window of usage statistics (e.g. over a day), then the eventGranularity field should be set accordingly.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.EditableDashboardPropertiesClass.created
documentation: An AuditStamp corresponding to the creation of this resource/association/sub-resource. A value of 0 for time indicates missing data.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.EditableDashboardPropertiesClass.lastModified
documentation: An AuditStamp corresponding to the last modification of this resource/association/sub-resource. If no modification has happened since creation, lastModified should be the same as created. A value of 0 for time indicates missing data.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.EditableDashboardPropertiesClass.deleted
documentation: An AuditStamp corresponding to the deletion of this resource/association/sub-resource. Logically, deleted MUST have a later timestamp than creation. It may or may not have the same time as lastModified depending upon the resource/association/sub-resource semantics.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.EditableDashboardPropertiesClass.description
documentation: Edited documentation of the dashboard

Stores editable changes made to properties. This separates changes made from
ingestion pipelines and edits in the UI to avoid accidental overwrites of user-provided data by ingestion pipelines

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.AzkabanJobTypeClass

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.AzkabanJobTypeClass.COMMAND
documentation:
The command job type is one of the basic built-in types. It runs multiple UNIX commands using java processbuilder.
Upon execution, Azkaban spawns off a process to run the command.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.AzkabanJobTypeClass.HADOOP_JAVA
documentation:
Runs a java program with ability to access Hadoop cluster.
https://azkaban.readthedocs.io/en/latest/jobTypes.html#java-job-type

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.AzkabanJobTypeClass.HADOOP_SHELL
documentation:
In large part, this is the same Command type. The difference is its ability to talk to a Hadoop cluster
securely, via Hadoop tokens.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.AzkabanJobTypeClass.HIVE
documentation: Hive type is for running Hive jobs.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.AzkabanJobTypeClass.PIG
documentation: Pig type is for running Pig jobs.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.AzkabanJobTypeClass.SQL
documentation: SQL is for running Presto, mysql queries etc

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.AzkabanJobTypeClass.GLUE
documentation: Glue type is for running AWS Glue job transforms.
The various types of support azkaban jobs

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetFieldProfileClass

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetFieldProfileClass.uniqueCount
documentation:
The count of unique values for the field across the dataset.
Useful for understanding the diversity of field values.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetFieldProfileClass.uniqueProportion
documentation:
The proportion of unique values over the total number of non-null entries.
Helps in assessing the uniqueness of the data in the field.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetFieldProfileClass.nullCount
documentation:
The count of null or missing entries for the field.
Important for data quality assessments.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetFieldProfileClass.nullProportion
documentation:
The proportion of null entries over the total number of entries in the field.
Useful for identifying fields with a high rate of missing values.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetFieldProfileClass.min
documentation:
The minimum value in the field for string-representable data types.
Provides a sense of the lower bound of field values.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetFieldProfileClass.max
documentation:
The maximum value in the field for string-representable data types.
Provides a sense of the upper bound of field values.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetFieldProfileClass.mean
documentation:
The mean (average) value of the field, represented as a string.
Useful for numerical fields to understand the central tendency.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetFieldProfileClass.median
documentation:
The median value of the field, represented as a string.
Useful for understanding the middle value when data is sorted.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetFieldProfileClass.stdev
documentation:
The standard deviation of the field values, represented as a string.
Indicates the amount of variation or dispersion of field values.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetFieldProfileClass.quantiles
documentation:
Only for numerical columns. Sorted list of quantile cutoffs for the field, in ascending order.
Helps in assessing the diversity of the data in the field.
Expect both the quantile and the value field to be string-ified floats.
Ex: quantile: "0.25", value: "1.0" means the 25th percentile of values falls at 1.0

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetFieldProfileClass.histogram
documentation:
Only for numerical columns. A histogram where a range of values in a numerical column
are rolled up into bins, with the heights representing the number of values in that bin.
Divided into k bin boundaries, with k-1 frequencies; first+last frequency values are tail weights
Currently unused in the product.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetFieldProfileClass.distinctValueFrequencies
documentation:
Volume of each column value for a low-cardinality / categorical field.
Useful for understanding the distribution of values in the field, and monitoring for changes
in specific value volumes.
For numeric colums, the value will contain a strigified value.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetFieldProfileClass.sampleValues
documentation: N randomly sampled values of the column. Useful for showing rough preview of the column.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetFieldProfileClass.fieldPath
documentation: The standard path to the field
Stats corresponding to fields in a dataset

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetMetricsOriginTypeClass.CHANGED_ROWS_QUERY

Issue a query to the table to obtain the metric values across
only the rows which have changed since the previous evaluation.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetMetricsSourceDetailsClass.originType

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetMetricsSourceDetailsClass.changedRowsField
documentation:
A field that can be used to filter for the fresh rows since the previous assertion
evaluation. This should be present when sourceType = CHANGED_ROWS_QUERY.

If not provided, or the metricSourceType is not QUERY, then the assertion will be evaluated against the
entire dataset.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetMetricsSourceDetailsClass.filter
documentation:
Any additional filters that were applied in a query to retrieve the metric.
Only possible to be present if originType is ALL_ROWS_QUERY or CHANGED_ROWS_QUERY.
Additional details about the origins of a captured source-platform Metric or Metric Set.

EditableDatasetProperties stores editable changes made to dataset properties. This separates changes made from
ingestion pipelines and edits in the UI to avoid accidental overwrites of user-provided data by ingestion pipelines

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.PartitionSummaryClass

documentation:
A unique id / value for the partition for which statistics were collected,
generated by applying the key definition to a given row.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.PartitionSummaryClass.createdTime
documentation: The created time for a given partition.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.PartitionSummaryClass.lastModifiedTime
documentation: The last modified / touched time for a given partition.
Defines how the data is partitioned

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.PartitionsSummaryClass

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.PartitionsSummaryClass.minPartition
documentation: The minimum partition as ordered

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.PartitionsSummaryClass.maxPartition
documentation: The maximum partition as ordered
Defines how the data is partitioned for Data Lake tables (e.g. Hive, S3, Iceberg, Delta, Hudi, etc).

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SchemaFieldProfileClass

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SchemaFieldProfileClass.uniqueCount
documentation:
The count of unique values for the field across the dataset.
Useful for understanding the diversity of field values.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SchemaFieldProfileClass.uniqueProportion
documentation:
The proportion of unique values over the total number of non-null entries.
Helps in assessing the uniqueness of the data in the field.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SchemaFieldProfileClass.nullCount
documentation:
The count of null or missing entries for the field.
Important for data quality assessments.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SchemaFieldProfileClass.nullProportion
documentation:
The proportion of null entries over the total number of entries in the field.
Useful for identifying fields with a high rate of missing values.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SchemaFieldProfileClass.min
documentation:
The minimum value in the field for string-representable data types.
Provides a sense of the lower bound of field values.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SchemaFieldProfileClass.max
documentation:
The maximum value in the field for string-representable data types.
Provides a sense of the upper bound of field values.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SchemaFieldProfileClass.mean
documentation:
The mean (average) value of the field, represented as a string.
Useful for numerical fields to understand the central tendency.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SchemaFieldProfileClass.median
documentation:
The median value of the field, represented as a string.
Useful for understanding the middle value when data is sorted.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SchemaFieldProfileClass.stdev
documentation:
The standard deviation of the field values, represented as a string.
Indicates the amount of variation or dispersion of field values.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SchemaFieldProfileClass.quantiles
documentation:
Only for numerical columns. Sorted list of quantile cutoffs for the field, in ascending order.
Helps in assessing the diversity of the data in the field.
Expect both the quantile and the value field to be string-ified floats.
Ex: quantile: "0.25", value: "1.0" means the 25th percentile of values falls at 1.0

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SchemaFieldProfileClass.histogram
documentation:
Only for numerical columns. A histogram where a range of values in a numerical column
are rolled up into bins, with the heights representing the number of values in that bin.
Divided into k bin boundaries, with k-1 frequencies; first+last frequency values are tail weights
Currently unused in the product.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SchemaFieldProfileClass.distinctValueFrequencies
documentation:
Volume of each column value for a low-cardinality / categorical field.
Useful for understanding the distribution of values in the field, and monitoring for changes
in specific value volumes.
For numeric colums, the value will contain a strigified value.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SchemaFieldProfileClass.sampleValues
documentation: N randomly sampled values of the column. Useful for showing rough preview of the column.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SchemaFieldProfileClass.timestampMillis
documentation: The event timestamp field as epoch at UTC in milli seconds.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SchemaFieldProfileClass.eventGranularity
documentation: Granularity of the event if applicable

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SchemaFieldProfileClass.partitionSpec
documentation: The optional partition specification.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SchemaFieldProfileClass.messageId
documentation: The optional messageId, if provided serves as a custom user-defined unique identifier for an aspect value.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.SchemaFieldProfileClass.source
documentation: Rich details about the source of the metrics provided in this profile.

Metrics corresponding to a single schema field.
Notice that this aspect may be partially completed so the user
should filter for existence of the stats they need when querying.

In the long term, this serves as a replacement for the DatasetFieldProfile object.
Note that it does not yet support quantiles, histograms, etc, but may evolve to in the future.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.NotificationRecipientClass.id

A unique channel / target id that the sink can pick up.
For slack sink, this will contain the channel or user id to send to.
For email sink, this will contain the email address to send to.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.NotificationRecipientClass.origin
documentation: The origin of the recipient notification, for tracking.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.NotificationRecipientClass.actor

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.NotificationRecipientClass.displayName
documentation: An optional display name to use when addressing the recipient. Available only for subscription or personal notifications.
The targeted recipient of a notification, either a specific User or a specific channel.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.NotificationRecipientOriginTypeClass.SUBSCRIPTION
documentation: The notification originated from a subscription.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.NotificationRecipientOriginTypeClass.GLOBAL_NOTIFICATION
documentation: The notification originated from a global change.

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.NotificationRecipientTypeClass

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.NotificationRecipientTypeClass.EMAIL
documentation: A specific email address to send a notification to.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.NotificationRecipientTypeClass.SLACK_CHANNEL
documentation: A slack channel to send a notification to.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.NotificationRecipientTypeClass.SLACK_DM
documentation: A slack member to send a notification to.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.NotificationRecipientTypeClass.CUSTOM
documentation:
A custom target, e.g. a slack channel or dm, that a specific sink can handle. Sinks are expected to handle custom
types appropriately.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.NotificationRecipientTypeClass.USER
documentation:
Deprecated! A DataHub user (via urn.). Currently this is NOT USED and may be removed in future versions.
Recipient type should correspond to the sink type, and the sink should understand how to handle the recipient type appropriately.
Instead, typically notifications have specific recipients as Slack handles, slack channels, or email addresses.

A type of target (recipient) channel for a notification. Typically a single recipient
maps to a single sink type. Each sink can support one or more recipient types.

Each type of Notification Sink exposes the recipient types that they support sinking to. This
is used by the notification manager to filter out recipients that are not supported by a given sink.

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ConditionClass

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ConditionClass.CONTAIN
documentation: Represent the relation: String field contains value, e.g. name contains Profile

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ConditionClass.END_WITH
documentation: Represent the relation: String field ends with value, e.g. name ends with Event

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ConditionClass.EQUAL
documentation: Represent the relation: field = value, e.g. platform = hdfs

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ConditionClass.IEQUAL
documentation: Represent the relation: field = value and support case insensitive values, e.g. platform = hdfs

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ConditionClass.IS_NULL
documentation: Represent the relation: field is null, e.g. platform is null

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ConditionClass.EXISTS
documentation: Represents the relation: field exists and is non-empty, e.g. owners is not null and != [] (empty)

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ConditionClass.GREATER_THAN
documentation: Represent the relation greater than, e.g. ownerCount > 5

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ConditionClass.GREATER_THAN_OR_EQUAL_TO
documentation: Represent the relation greater than or equal to, e.g. ownerCount >= 5

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ConditionClass.IN
documentation: Represent the relation: String field is one of the array values to, e.g. name in ["Profile", "Event"]

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ConditionClass.LESS_THAN
documentation: Represent the relation less than, e.g. ownerCount < 3

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ConditionClass.LESS_THAN_OR_EQUAL_TO
documentation: Represent the relation less than or equal to, e.g. ownerCount <= 3

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ConditionClass.START_WITH
documentation: Represent the relation: String field starts with value, e.g. name starts with PageView

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ConditionClass.DESCENDANTS_INCL
documentation: Represent the relation: URN field any nested children in addition to the given URN

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ConditionClass.ANCESTORS_INCL
documentation: Represent the relation: URN field matches any nested parent in addition to the given URN

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.ConditionClass.RELATED_INCL
documentation: Represent the relation: URN field matches any nested child or parent in addition to the given URN
The matching condition in a filter criterion

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.CriterionClass.values
documentation:

Note, if values is set, the above "value" field will be ignored

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.CriterionClass.condition
documentation: The condition for the criterion, e.g. EQUAL, START_WITH

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.CriterionClass.negated
documentation: Whether the condition should be negated
A criterion for matching a field with given value

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FilterClass

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FilterClass.or_
documentation: A list of disjunctive criterion for the filter. (or operation to combine filters)

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.FilterClass.criteria
documentation: Deprecated! A list of conjunctive criterion for the filter. If "or" field is provided, then this field is ignored.
The filter for finding a record or a collection of records

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLModelFactorsClass.groups
documentation:
Groups refers to distinct categories with similar characteristics that are present in the evaluation data instances.
For human-centric machine learning MLModels, groups are people who share one or multiple characteristics.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLModelFactorsClass.instrumentation
documentation:
The performance of a MLModel can vary depending on what instruments were used to capture the input to the MLModel.
For example, a face detection model may perform differently depending on the camera's hardware and software,
including lens, image stabilization, high dynamic range techniques, and background blurring for portrait mode.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.MLModelFactorsClass.environment

Quantitative analyses should be disaggregated, that is, broken down by the chosen factors. Quantitative analyses should provide the results of evaluating the MLModel according to the chosen metrics, providing confidence interval values when possible.

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.TrainingDataClass

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.TrainingDataClass.trainingData
documentation: Details on the dataset(s) used for training the MLModel

Ideally, the MLModel card would contain as much information about the training data as the evaluation data. However, there might be cases where it is not feasible to provide this level of detailed information about the training data. For example, the data may be proprietary, or require a non-disclosure agreement. In these cases, we advocate for basic details about the distributions over groups in the data, as well as any other details that could inform stakeholders on the kinds of biases the model may have encoded.

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.AssertionMonitorCapabilityClass

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.AssertionMonitorCapabilityClass.ASSERTION_EVALUATION
documentation:
Whether the metrics used for evaluating an assertion should be collected and saved. These metrics
can be used for training, debugging, and even display in other contexts of the application.

This is particularly useful for smart assertion evaluation, where the assertion is evaluated based on the metrics collected.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.AssertionMonitorCapabilityClass.METRIC_COLLECTION
documentation:
Whether the metrics used for evaluating an assertion should be collected and saved in a shared, globally accessible location (e.g. asset profiles).
These metrics can be used for training, debugging, and even display in other contexts of the application.

This is particularly useful for offline smart assertion training, where the smart assertion inference pipeline
is de-coupled process that trains and updates the assertion itself based on historical metric values.
Individual toggle-able capability for an assertion monitor.

The source of the information used to evaluate
a column values or metric assertion

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetFieldAssertionParametersClass.changedRowsField
documentation:
A field that can be used to filter for the fresh rows since the previous assertion
evaluation. This should be present when sourceType = CHANGED_ROWS_QUERY.

If not provided, or the metricSourceType is not QUERY, then the assertion will be evaluated against the
entire dataset.
Information about the parameters required to evaluate a field / column assertion

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetFieldAssertionSourceTypeClass.ALL_ROWS_QUERY
documentation:
Issue a query to the table to obtain the metric or values across
all rows.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetFieldAssertionSourceTypeClass.CHANGED_ROWS_QUERY
documentation:
Issue a query to the table to obtain the metric values across
only the rows which have changed since the previous evaluation.

class_variables.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.DatasetFieldAssertionSourceTypeClass.DATAHUB_DATASET_PROFILE
documentation:
Determine the metric value using the DataHub dataset profile. Only applicable
for Column Metric assertions, not Column Value assertions.

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.PlatformResourceInfoClass

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.PlatformResourceInfoClass.resourceType
documentation:
The type of the resource. 
Intended as a loose specifier of the generic type of the resource.
Producer is not forced to conform to a specific set of symbols for
resource types.
The @PlatformResourceType enumeration offers a paved path for agreed upon
common terms, but is not required to be followed.
Example values could be: conversation, user, grant, etc.
Resource types are indexed for ease of access. 
e.g. Get me all platform resources of type user for the platform looker

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.PlatformResourceInfoClass.primaryKey
documentation:
The primary key for this platform resource.
e.g. for a slack member this would be the memberID.
primary keys specified here don't need to include any additional specificity for the
     dataPlatform
The @PlatformResourceKey is supposed to represent that

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.PlatformResourceInfoClass.secondaryKeys
documentation:
The secondary keys this platform resource can be located by.
I.e., for a slack member this would be email or phone.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.PlatformResourceInfoClass.value
documentation: The serialized value of this platform resource item.

Platform Resource Info.
These entities are for miscelaneous data that is used in non-core parts of the system.
For instance, if we want to persist & retrieve data from auxiliary integrations such as Slack or Microsoft Teams.

classes.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.PlatformResourceKeyClass

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.PlatformResourceKeyClass.id
documentation:
A unique id for this entity.
There are no constraints on the format of this id, but most implementations
will choose to use a UUID.
This id should be globally unique for the entire DataHub instance and
     uniquely identify the resource that is being stored, so most
     implementations
will combine logical attributes like platform name, platform instance,
platform-specific-id and the resource type to create the unique id.
e.g. slack:slack-instance:slack-user-id:user-info 
or guid(slack, slack-instance, slack-user-id, user-info) etc.

Key for a Platform Resource.
Platform Resources are assets that are not part of the core data model.
They are stored in DataHub primarily to help with application-specific
use-cases that are not sufficiently generalized to move into the core data model.
For instance, if we want to persist & retrieve additional user profile data 
from auxiliary integrations such as Slack or Microsoft Teams for resolving details later.

documentation:
The value type of the property. Must be a dataType.
e.g. To indicate that the property is of type DATE, use urn:li:dataType:datahub.date

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.StructuredPropertyDefinitionClass.typeQualifier
documentation:
A map that allows for type specialization of the valueType.
e.g. a valueType of urn:li:dataType:datahub.urn
can be specialized to be a USER or GROUP URN by adding a typeQualifier like 
{ "allowedTypes": ["urn:li:entityType:datahub.corpuser", "urn:li:entityType:datahub.corpGroup"] }

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.StructuredPropertyDefinitionClass.allowedValues
documentation:
A list of allowed values that the property is allowed to take. 
If this is not specified, then the property can take any value of given type.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.StructuredPropertyDefinitionClass.cardinality
documentation: The cardinality of the property. If not specified, then the property is assumed to be single valued..

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.StructuredPropertyDefinitionClass.description
documentation: The description of the property. This is the description that will be shown in the UI.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.StructuredPropertyDefinitionClass.searchConfiguration

Search configuration for this property. If not specified, then the property is indexed using the default mapping.
from the logical type.

documentation: Whether the structured property value is immutable once applied to an entity.

methods.name: acryl_datahub_cloud.metadata.schema_classes.StructuredPropertyDefinitionClass.version
documentation:
Definition version - Allows breaking schema changes. String is compared case-insensitive and new
versions must be monotonically increasing. Cannot use periods/dots.
Suggestions: v1, v2
             20240610, 20240611
